reviews.review_text:
Cash grab game. 2/3s of the game is behind DLC, and so is FIRST PERSON MODE. Who makes a game, and puts FIRST PERSON MODE BEHIND A PAYWALL? Disgusting.

Got it for £5.49, refunded.
Saber Interactive's second best horde killing game behind Space Marine 2. Bots (and NPC AIs) are even ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ than their most recent Toxic Commando which is the only con I have with this.

Im gonna refund the game, i had to download some files elsewhere and add them to the games folder to stop it flickering, then it ran fine, although every time theres an explosion the game crashes meaning its unplayable.

best L4D2 type game even better i say. The graphics, animations, survivor and zombie textures, voice acting all perfect and well optimized. Got this free back in the day on Epic and just got to buy it just to support the devs.

I played this for roughly 20 minutes, and the gameplay instantly felt familiar. You may take that as either a positive or a negative after what I'm about to say. I could go on and on about all the specifics, but I really don't want to play the game long enough to make that type of review. To save us both some time all you need to know is, it's a worse version of L4D2. 

For some, the similarity to L4D2 may be a positive thing, and for those people that will be enough to make a purchase. As for me, I regret it - even though I bought it with a steep discount. I just don't see the point in playing L4D2 at home, when I already have L4D2. I won't be recommending it, but for some people it will probably be a good time, especially with friends or a group of good people. At minimum, I suggest doing plenty of research before deciding whether you want to purchase this.

v---edit-----v

I almost forgot, it may or may not be worth mentioning this but, the game features an FPS mode that is, for some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ reason, locked behind the Aftermath edition. Might be worth consideration, especially if you plan to buy this game

This game is a pretty good game, but I can also see this game not being for everyone. This game simulates the endless hordes of world war Z pretty well, you have to fight against waves of hundreds or thousands of zombies and coordinate with your team members in order to do so. However, the game is just that mowing down hundreds of zombies, it has little else in terms of content, as it only has 9 episodes consisting of 3 or 4 missions to play through. and its very minor on story in those episodes, you just get some background information that's really about it. Once you get through those it will be just you replaying the same missions over and over, but what makes this so fun is because its just a really fun time waster mowing down hundreds of zombies. Of course you wont like this game if that isn't your kind of game, doesn't mean its bad though. But I can definitely see why this game isn't really all that popular.

But basically the game consists of moving through a level and then reaching defense sections where you set up defense kits around the area. and then holding of a swarm that consists of a few hundred zombies. You will only have a limited amount of time to set up defenses, as the swarm will not wait for you and will eventually arrive on its own. and sometimes zombies will come up behind you during these swarms to test if you're watching your flanks. rinse and repeat. There are also special zombies which will also test to make sure you're paying attention and watching your corners, as they like to ambush you if you run around corners too quickly without checking your corners. Also this game encourages scavenging for loot, it likes to hide loot in obscure locations which if you can find these you get rewarded with two items. The first are breaching charges to open up special doors which are basically like loot rooms, and virus samples that give you bonus EXP and coins if you can successfully carry them to the end of a mission without it breaking from too much damage. its a very good time waster. Once you get through all 9 episodes you can then either repeat these missions endlessly or play horde mode. 

Horde mode is an endless mode that sees you surviving for as long as possible against the never ending swarms of hundreds of zombies until you lose. You gain points every round to spend in the shop to either upgrade your weapons, buy medical items, or defense kits. The only annoying thing is you drop all of your points on the floor when you are killed, meaning other team members can steal your points when you die. They also have an XL version where if your PC can handle it, is swarms of thousands of zombies instead.

The game has 8 different classes for you to play as, and each bring their own ways to help out your team whether that being the team's medic to keep them alive, or being a DPS class to deal damage and take down the zombies such as gunslinger, hellraiser or exterminator.  or a support class to help the DPS classes take down those zombies, such as fixer which can deploy ammo boxes to provide your team with explosive rounds, or dronemaster which helps against the special zombies.

The game also has friendly fire, so you have to watch where you're aiming or you will drill your teammates in the back. And the game doesn't joke around with friendly fire, especially on the higher difficulties where one shot can drain like 30-50% of your teammates HP. 

So you basically just repeat this cycle over and over until you get bored of mowing down hundreds of zombies. which if that is your kind of thing you will like this game, if it is not then you probably will not like this game. It is definitely my thing though, as I absolutely cannot get enough of just mowing down hundreds of zombies, its an insanely fun thing to do.

I’ve played this game for nearly 1,800 hours, so I am one of the most qualified to speak on it. My evaluation of it is somewhere between good and bad. Why do I say that? Because the maps are boring; some of them are monotonous and not fun at all. When you play them for the first time, there might be some enjoyment, but after playing them a few more times, they become repetitive and dull. The game menu itself displays a model of the Earth, so why not release more global maps? The gameplay could also be more flexible. For example, at the beginning, the four teammates could be separated and placed in different corners of the game world, representing how several characters survive and regroup when the apocalypse comes, and then break through the enemy lines. You could take inspiration from popular games like Call of Duty, Battlefield, and The Last of Us. This game could be popular worldwide, but the developers are incompetent! If all else fails, hire me as your development and creative director—I can provide you with an outline!
Good game to play and def will reco to others to play. More content and weapons lads.

Was looking for a cross platform game that I could play with my brother who is living far away currently. The game was on sale, had good reviews, fit the criteria of other games we liked (L4D2, Day Z) , and my brother had it on game pass so I bought it. However, I only bought the base game without DLC...

Now I am wishing that I had bought all of the content available in one go. Even if you have no clue what World War Z is lore wise, this game is amazing as a stand alone co-op game to play with friends. It is as close as Left 4 Dead 3 as it gets. The only thing different is it follows some of the stories from the original book and the movie, I believe, and has DLC content for other stories.

Overall this is a GREAT co-op game. Do not buy this if you are going to play alone with A.I. teammates. The gun play is great, the skins make it feel like you are upgrading your weapons and adding attachments as you level up, the characters and classes are a fun addition, and the zombies stacking up like they did in the movie is so awesome. It is well optimized also, runs smoothly on my PC (see specs).

If you are looking for a thrilling time with your friends going on adventures in different stories throughout the world fighting hordes of zombies and saving people, than this is your game.

Dude, imagine locking first person mode behind a DLC. Disgusting.

The game is trying to be something it isn't, and it's failing miserably. The guns feel awful and not even the sounds are synced with the animations. Went in expecting nothing and was still disappointed.

I gave this game a try during the promotion and holy, I wouldn't even play this if I got it for free. The guns feel absolutely terrible to use. Movement feels clunky and lots of the customization options are just a different type of hat or recolor.
The fixing about flickering and bug glitches on IGPU is still not resolved even after almost few years back ..I had no problem with Aftermath cracked version.

+It's fun, it's rather cinematic, not as in many animations but you can see a huge amount of zombies spawning flooding your screen as if they were a river going towards you, they also climb walls like in the movie.

-do try to at least play the game with 3 or 4 players. the bots are atrocious, getting in front of you, blocking your bullets and suffering friendly damage, once a player falls they go immediately to pick you up and die immediately because they are surrounded by zombies. They also love getting close and personal with the special zombies which usually means they will die.

-some special zombies are really annoying to fight against, the bomber one runs towards you silently until you shoot at it, so if you don't see him before hand it will creep up to you and explode in front of you, kill you and your teammates, the creeper spawns in front of you when you are alone so don't go on your own. And the rats... you better hope one player has grenades or explosive ammo or an explosive heavy because you will lose over half your health otherwise.

Annoying at times and very random with how the special enemies and equipment spawn. Sometimes you get lucky other times you get 2 tank zombies at the same time with no healing whatsoever. But it's fun.
Its all about shooting zombies! Episodes get a bit repetitive, but the twists in each chapters actually makes them unique and fun to play with friends.

An interesting game a bit different from the movie with how the zombies act. I finished every free episode. I don't like how I have to pay for more episodes after already purchasing the game. Although Its a 4.5 for me, I am a huge fan of the movie. Maybe a good episode idea would be to add the storyline in the movie along with the characters in every chapter of the movie. Overall it's an amazing game worth buying if you love zombies and shooter games. I also noticed it is quite a bit like Left 4 Dead which is also an other game worth trying.

Horde mode is insane and replayable, the campaigns not so much.

Grindy. Class customization is what separates it from other Zombie shooters.

AI teammates suck which makes the game playable only when you're with other real players.

Absolutely fantastic gunplay, very solid mission design, and awesome (if not the same level as LFD2) gore effects.
Actually playing through the game is awesome and highly satisfying. But man it feels a completely different team designed the class progression, weapon and currency sytem. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ this is a grind- I cannot believe how grindy this game is considering its a simple PvE zombie horse shooter. Also, too many dlcs- there are like 4 main chapters in the base game and 5 more that are locked behind DLCs. 
It's a genuinely fun game but I cannot belive how greedy the devs/publishers (or whever are behind this) are.
I recommend the Aftermath edition on sale cuz itss too expensive otherwise (if you buy the standard version and rest of the DLCs separately)

A very good game overall would recommend it to play with friends, i would say get the game only if its on a huge sale like 80-70% off. overall a very fun game to play, this a game u can have in your library and play when whenever you feel like it and it will always be fun even if you playing it again and again.
Surprised by this really fast-paced fun game with great community
It is a super fun game it may be grindy can be difficult at times but its still fun need more content but overall the game is great

The game is really good overall, the perk system is solid, and the campaign is a lot of fun. My main issue is the community. Whenever I join Campaign, Horde, or Horde XL, there are always some level 500+ or level 999 players who kill me for no reason, even when I haven’t done anything.

Really fun, even with just one other friend and bots filling the rest. I suggest watching a basic tutorial on how the perks and extras work. I wasted a ton of in game currency not knowing how it was working.

You liked left 4 dead 2? You wanted more but it never came to be? Back 4 blood, a "spiritual successor" didn't meet your expectations? Well, you're in luck as this here game offers a bit of a different approach to your typical zombie shooter. What's good about every PvE? A lot of things come to mind, but for me personally it's the incoming hordes of enemies storming you and your team. It's something that this game does exceptionally well. Forget hordes in l4d2, Deep Rock Galactic bug swarms, fighting off assaults in Payday - WWZ offers you a REAL swarm of enemies and let me tell you, if what you seek is a challenge then this game might be just for you. When just a single common zombie can seriously mess you up, how will you deal with hundreds of them at a time? The answer is simple: guns, big, bad, mean guns. In this game you will fight a literal war against the Zeekes. The question is, will you survive? Bring your own toys to the battlefield or scavenge them on your way through the level but more importantly bring friends you can rely on. Shooting each other in the back, none of you will come out of it alive. Also keep in mind that once you're pinned down, there's no getting back up on your own.

I swear Saber Interactive never misses! This is actually my 4th time buying the game, as I bought it for my OG Left 4 Dead 2 trio back in 2019 on the PS4. We unfortunately never hit it off, but hey, I did have a blast playing it with a buddy of mine on PC. Lmao, the AIs are so incompetent when it comes to assisting us, and we’d probably up the difficulty if we had more people, but this game was still a lot of fun nonetheless. It's honestly a better spiritual successor to the Left 4 Dead series than the trash that is Back 4 Blood.

My buddy and I were grinding the hell out of Z and decided to get the Aftermath DLC for more content and the first-person perspective. Well, the content’s great, but that “immersive new first-person mode” sucks! It's not even a true first-person experience. It's basically the CS:GO version of first-person mode, as you can't even ADS your guns. I thought I was tripping at first, but no, they just zoom you in a bit this time, which, in my opinion, is ridiculous since the game's guns feel and sound amazing. I didn't refund the DLC, but be mindful before you buy it.

All in all, a great game that I can't wait to return to with an actual squad. Oh, it's also one of the rare occasions of a good movie having a good game as well. They really oughta nerf Brad Pitt and I ;)

Interestingly, this game is like a mix between Left 4 Dead and Killing Floor. You have classes, but they don't really define the guns you have access to. They do give you a different default weapon each but outside of that the class levels are more about defining your special ability as opposed to selecting a specific weapon style. You can realistically use whatever weapons you find across the stages without penalty. 

Additionally, you have multiple stages to every level, but there is a match end at the every stage where you can pause, get a new team, character, or whatever. Like those other games, there is some kind of a story for every level but not really. Its largely just, "survive till the evacuation!" "fill the gas tank!" "escort the VIP to the end and defend them!" 

You also have an account level too but honestly I haven't seen anything really worth investing in on that front. Little cosmetics, title card banners, stuff like that. 

About the only ding that I could find was with the number of DLC purchases you need to make. The base game is really small and its not worth full price at all. The aftermath expansion is a wise investment, but you still have to buy two more map packs for the full experience. At 4 hours played, I've gone through about half the base game on Normal and I probably won't be picking up anymore DLC unless the later stuff I unlocked with Aftermath is a significant improvement. 

Overall, I picked it up as a part of a combo with Toxic Commando and I find it significantly more enjoyable. I would recommend buying it on sale, yeah.
amazing zombie shooter, nice physics ad details in game. well done.
if u like hug zombies horde with chainsaw like l4d2 and esacping from tham in finale mission with boat or with helicopter u Definitely will like that game get it on sale ! 9/10 <3

This is a fun Zombie Survival game. Not better than L4D2, but more challenging (which I like) so I'll give it that. Really like the upgradable firearms, a good verity of perks for anyone's liking, and a good amount of groups of characters to choose from (They may not be as memorable as the L4D1/2 characters but it's something I like to add), but gotta like the Bodies reference (Bodies by drowning pool (2001)) from the Italian girl with the motorcycle helmet. Anyways, and the last thing I like about this game is that this game can be played offline. Why I bring this up? Is for those that stopped playing online (like myself) and or have terrible wifi. 

But this game is not a 10/10, I had some issues. 

1st, the bots are react slow when pinned down by a mutated zombie (most of the time in hoard mode unless I'm next to them)
2nd, (so far, this happened twice) when I climb over the fence, I glitched through the floor and go outside the map, good thing it's just 3-7 seconds long before I get back up. Hoard mode, not a big deal, I'll just get around the fence, but in the campaign maps where I have to climb over the fence to get to my current destination, it can be annoying. But again, this only happened twice so far. A simple restart can fix that, for a while anyway. 
 
Overall, good game if you're into Zombie Survival.
